notes: Lesson 18 Part 1 (act 104), 'Converging pointers', 94.9s, 6 short cues. Concrete converging-pointer walkthrough over the sorted array [1,3,4,6,8,11], target 10: L starts on the smallest, R on the largest; each sum-vs-target comparison advances exactly one pointer inward, collapsing the O(n^2) all-pairs search to a single O(n) sweep. Sum trace: 1+11=12 (over, drop right) -> 1+8=9 (under, push left) -> 3+8=11 (over, drop right) -> 3+6=9 (under, push left) -> 4+6=10 (found at indices 2,3). Uses arrays.py idioms (value_row, recolor_cell, pointer, value_badge, complexity); MathTex reserved for the O(n^2)->O(n) bound. Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke every cue, then full -ql render (6 chunks 854x480x15, niced/serial) + READ start/mid/end for all 6 cues. Iteration 2 fixed the target chip overlapping the wide value row's last cell (moved it to the upper-right, clear of the row) and re-rendered + re-verified. Per-cue checklist (label legibility + math correctness, highlighted element matches the beat, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 94.801s vs audio 94.896s (delta 0.095s, within tolerance).
